Part B.key_evidence: Reference image is heavily saturated/masked (white blob) due to a very bright PS1 neighbor (sgscore1=0.95, srmag1=13.5) at 4.28 arcsec. Science shows two bright sources, and the difference image displays a complex residual at the center rather than a clean PSF-like positive residual. ndethist=1 with ncovhist=1319 indicates this position has been observed many times but only detected once. classtar=0.019 is unreliable here given saturation.
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: The candidate sits adjacent to a saturated bright star whose reference is masked, producing poor subtractions. The messy difference residual and proximity to a 13.5 mag star (distpsnr1=4.28") strongly suggest a subtraction artifact from the saturated neighbor rather than a real new transient. This is classified as bogus/artifact.
Part B.alternative_analysis: An alternative is a real stellar variable companion or flare near the bright star, which could explain a positive residual at first detection. However, the difference morphology is irregular and not a clean PSF, and the dominance of the saturated neighbor makes a clean astrophysical interpretation unlikely from this single epoch.
